title: Grade 3 Math
description: STAAR Grade 3 Math curriculum aligned to the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills (TEKS). Topics are organized in teaching order from foundational number sense through data analysis and personal financial literacy.

units.title: Place Value & Whole Numbers
units.summary: Read, write, compare, and round whole numbers up to 100,000 using place value.

lessons.title: Place value to 100,000
lessons.objective: Compose and decompose numbers up to 100,000 using place value.

questions.prompt: Which number has a 7 in the thousands place?
questions.explanation: In 47,000, the digit 7 is in the thousands place. The 4 is in the ten-thousands place.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the digit 5 in 35,621?
questions.explanation: The digit 5 sits in the thousands place, so its value is 5 × 1,000 = 5,000.

lessons.title: Comparing & ordering whole numbers
lessons.objective: Compare and order whole numbers up to 100,000 using >, <, and =.

questions.prompt: Which symbol makes this statement true?  42,318 ___ 42,381
questions.explanation: The thousands and hundreds digits match. In the tens place, 1 < 8, so 42,318 < 42,381.

lessons.title: Rounding to nearest 10 and 100
lessons.objective: Round to the nearest 10 or 100 to estimate.

questions.prompt: Round 467 to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: Look at the tens digit: 6. Since 6 ≥ 5, round up. 467 rounds to 500.

units.title: Fractions
units.summary: Represent, compare, and find equivalent fractions using models and number lines.

lessons.title: Fractions as parts of a whole
lessons.objective: Represent fractions of halves, fourths, and eighths as distances on a number line.

questions.prompt: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Maya eats 3 slices. What fraction did she eat?
questions.explanation: The whole has 8 equal parts (denominator). Maya ate 3 of them (numerator). So she ate 3/8.

lessons.title: Equivalent fractions
lessons.objective: Represent equivalent fractions with denominators of 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

questions.prompt: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
questions.explanation: 1/2 means one of two equal parts. 4/8 means four of eight equal parts — the same amount of the whole.

lessons.title: Comparing fractions
lessons.objective: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or same denominator.

questions.prompt: Which fraction is greater?  3/4 or 3/8
questions.explanation: When numerators are the same, the fraction with the smaller denominator is larger because each piece is bigger. So 3/4 > 3/8.

units.title: Addition & Subtraction within 1,000
units.summary: Add and subtract whole numbers fluently using strategies based on place value.

lessons.title: Adding 3-digit numbers
lessons.objective: Solve one- and two-step problems involving addition within 1,000.

questions.prompt: A library has 348 fiction books and 276 non-fiction books. How many books in all?
questions.explanation: Add 348 + 276. Ones: 8+6=14, write 4 carry 1. Tens: 4+7+1=12, write 2 carry 1. Hundreds: 3+2+1=6. Total: 624.

lessons.title: Subtracting with regrouping
lessons.objective: Subtract within 1,000 with regrouping.

questions.prompt: A store had 502 toys. They sold 168. How many are left?
questions.explanation: 502 − 168. Regroup: 502 becomes 4 hundreds, 9 tens, 12 ones. 12−8=4, 9−6=3, 4−1=3. Answer: 334.

units.title: Multiplication
units.summary: Build multiplication facts through 10×10 using arrays, equal groups, and properties.

lessons.title: Equal groups & arrays
lessons.objective: Determine the total number of objects when arranged in arrays or equal groups.

questions.prompt: Liam has 4 bags with 6 marbles in each. Which expression shows the total?
questions.explanation: Equal groups means multiplication. 4 groups of 6 = 4 × 6 = 24 marbles.

lessons.title: Multiplication facts to 10×10
lessons.objective: Recall multiplication facts up to 10 × 10.

questions.prompt: What is 7 × 8?

lessons.title: Word problems with multiplication
lessons.objective: Solve one- and two-step problems involving multiplication.

questions.prompt: A classroom has 6 rows of desks with 5 desks in each row. How many desks total?
questions.explanation: Rows × desks per row = total. 6 × 5 = 30 desks.

units.title: Division
units.summary: Divide using equal sharing and equal groups, and connect division to multiplication.

lessons.title: Division as equal sharing
lessons.objective: Determine the number of objects in each group when sharing equally.

questions.prompt: 24 cookies are shared equally among 4 friends. How many cookies does each friend get?
questions.explanation: Equal sharing → division. 24 ÷ 4 = 6. Each friend gets 6 cookies.

lessons.title: Multiplication & division fact families
lessons.objective: Use the relationship between multiplication and division to solve problems.

questions.prompt: If 7 × 8 = 56, what is 56 ÷ 8?
questions.explanation: Multiplication and division are inverses. 7 × 8 = 56 means 56 ÷ 8 = 7.

units.title: Algebraic Reasoning & Patterns
units.summary: Represent and solve problems with equations, and identify number patterns.

lessons.title: Finding an unknown number
lessons.objective: Determine the unknown whole number in an equation.

questions.prompt: What number makes this equation true?  □ + 17 = 45
questions.explanation: Use the inverse: 45 − 17 = 28. Check: 28 + 17 = 45. ✓

lessons.title: Number patterns
lessons.objective: Identify and continue patterns in tables.

questions.prompt: Continue the pattern: 4, 8, 12, 16, ___
questions.explanation: Each number increases by 4. 16 + 4 = 20.

units.title: Geometry: 2D Shapes
units.summary: Classify polygons and quadrilaterals by attributes such as sides and angles.

lessons.title: Classifying polygons
lessons.objective: Classify and sort 2D shapes based on attributes.

questions.prompt: Which shape has exactly 4 sides and 4 right angles, with all sides the same length?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 4 right angles but sides may differ. A square has 4 equal sides AND 4 right angles.

lessons.title: Quadrilaterals
lessons.objective: Identify trapezoids, rhombuses, parallelograms, rectangles, and squares.

questions.prompt: Which is NOT a quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral has 4 sides. A pentagon has 5 sides, so it's not a quadrilateral.

units.title: Measurement: Perimeter & Area
units.summary: Find perimeter of polygons and area of rectangles.

lessons.title: Perimeter of polygons
lessons.objective: Find the perimeter of a polygon by adding side lengths.

questions.prompt: A rectangle has length 8 cm and width 5 cm. What is its perimeter in cm?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = sum of all sides = 8 + 5 + 8 + 5 = 26 cm. Or 2 × (8 + 5) = 26 cm.

lessons.title: Area of rectangles
lessons.objective: Find the area of rectangles by tiling and using length × width.

questions.prompt: What is the area of a rectangle with length 7 ft and width 4 ft? (square feet)
questions.explanation: Area of rectangle = length × width = 7 × 4 = 28 square feet.

units.title: Time, Liquid Volume & Weight
units.summary: Tell time to the minute, and measure liquid volume and weight in customary and metric units.

lessons.title: Elapsed time
lessons.objective: Determine elapsed time in minutes within a 24-hour period.

questions.prompt: A movie starts at 3:15 PM and ends at 4:50 PM. How many minutes long is the movie?
questions.explanation: From 3:15 to 4:15 is 60 min. From 4:15 to 4:50 is 35 min. Total: 60 + 35 = 95 minutes.

lessons.title: Liquid volume & weight
lessons.objective: Determine liquid volume (capacity) and weight using customary and metric units.

questions.prompt: Which unit best measures the weight of an apple?
questions.explanation: An apple is light, around 150–200 grams. Kilograms measure heavier objects; liters measure liquids; meters measure length.

units.title: Data Analysis
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Read and interpret frequency tables, dot plots, pictographs, and bar graphs with scaled intervals.

lessons.title: Reading bar graphs
lessons.objective: Summarize a data set using a frequency table, dot plot, pictograph, or bar graph.

questions.prompt: A bar graph shows: Apples=12, Bananas=8, Oranges=15, Grapes=6. Which fruit was chosen most?
questions.explanation: The largest value is 15 (Oranges). The bar for Oranges would be the tallest.

units.title: Personal Financial Literacy
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Identify income sources, distinguish needs vs wants, and understand saving and credit.

lessons.title: Needs vs wants
lessons.objective: Distinguish between fixed and variable expenses, and needs versus wants.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a NEED, not a want?
questions.explanation: A need is something required to live (food, water, shelter). Toys, games, and candy are wants — nice to have but not necessary.

lessons.title: Saving & credit
lessons.objective: Calculate how much money is saved after a period of time.

questions.prompt: Sara saves $5 each week. How much will she save in 8 weeks? (in dollars)
questions.explanation: Weekly amount × number of weeks = total saved. $5 × 8 = $40.
